title: Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – City of Newburgh

communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the City of Newburgh, Orange County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the City of Newburgh.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the City of Newburgh.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the City of Newburgh and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the City and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to city officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Mayor  
• City Council members  
• City of Newburgh Police Department personnel  
• City administrative staff  
• City legal counsel  
• Public Works or Planning Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the City of Newburgh and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Orange County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the City of Newburgh.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the city  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from City Council meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

communications.communication:

the total number of people by sex who are currently civilly committed in North Dakota for sex offenses ("sexually violent predators" or sex offenders). 

I request this data for the most recent date available. If this data is not available by sex, I request the total numbers regardless of sex.

Sincerely,

Emily Widra

title: AER - FL Agency for Healthcare Admin.

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: AER - FL Agency for Healthcare Admin.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse event records filed with the Florida Agency for Healthcare Administration between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.communication:

I would like to request information related to the project “Re-Thickening Arctic Sea Ice” also known as "RASI." I would like to request copies of any of the following documents related to that project created or modified since 1/1/2024:
Any risk management plans, risk registers or risk assessments
Any plans related to community engagement or consultations.
Any permit, license, or applications related to the proposed experiment

title: Jared Shadeed Missing Persons Case

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Jared Shadeed Missing Persons Case
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

from the Los Angeles Police Department regarding the missing persons
case of Jared Shadeed, who was last seen leaving an Airbnb on July 30 and whose car was found
abandoned in Los Angeles’ northeast area on August 17. I request the following files and will
accept any that are available to me.
- Full and complete case file regarding the missing persons case of Jared Shadeed
- Initial incident report - Any and all audio and video files related to the case
- Photographs or visual evidence related to the case
- Witness and family statements and interviews
- Field notes
I request a response time of five business days. If access will take longer, please provide an
estimated timeframe. As a member of the media pursuing this request for news-gathering
purposes in the public interest, I request a waiver of all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please
inform me if costs will exceed $250.
If you deny any portion, or all, of this request, please provide me with a written explanation of
the reason(s) for your denial, including a citation to each specific statutory exemption you feel
justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to
me under the law. If you conclude that portions of the records that I request are exempt from
disclosure, please release the remainder of such records for inspection and copying, redacting
only the portion or portions that you claim are exempt.
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at char.adams@audiochuck.com
or (463)-220-3997. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Courteney Stuart

title: George W. Bush Presidential Library: Records Regarding Russian Cyber Operations and "Patriotic Hacker" Coordination During the 2008 Conflict in Georgia

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: George W. Bush Presidential Library: Records Regarding Russian Cyber Operations and "Patriotic Hacker" Coordination During the 2008 Conflict in Georgia
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552. I am seeking access to and copies of records created or received by the National Security Council (NSC) and the Office of the Vice President (OVP) between August 1, 2008, and January 20, 2009.

Specifically, I am requesting all memoranda, situation reports, and briefing materials regarding the following:

Intelligence assessments of the "StopGeorgia.ru" forum and its role in coordinating Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks against Georgian government infrastructure.

Communications regarding the Russian Business Network (RBN) and its alleged links to Russian state actors or military intelligence (GRU) during the August 2008 hostilities.

Evaluations of "civilian-front" cyber operations (often termed "Patriotic Hackers") and whether these entities were identified as state-directed or state-facilitated cutouts.

National Security Council deliberations regarding the 2008 "Cyber Left Hook" and the resulting U.S. policy on "Cyber Neutrality" as it pertained to assisting the Georgian government’s digital migration.

Key Officials of Interest:
Records involving the following individuals within the Executive Office of the President are likely to be most responsive:

Stephen Hadley: National Security Advisor.
James Jeffrey: Deputy National Security Advisor.
Melissa Hathaway: Senior Director for Cyberspace (NSC).
Ed Gillespie: White House Counselor (regarding communications strategy on Russian disinformation).
John Tefft: U.S. Ambassador to Georgia (regarding reporting on infrastructure collapse).

If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a Vaughn Index or equivalent justification. I look forward to your response within the 20-day statutory period.

Thank you for the Bush Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: 2014–2016 Election Infrastructure DNS Vulnerabilities and Cyber Indicators

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: 2014–2016 Election Infrastructure DNS Vulnerabilities and Cyber Indicators
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request access to and copies of records created, received, or maintained by the National Security Council (NSC), the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP), and the White House Cybersecurity Coordinator between January 1, 2014, and January 20, 2017.

**Scope of Request**

I specifically request all records, including but not limited to memoranda, briefing materials, situation reports, emails, attachments, technical analyses, and interagency communications, concerning:

1. Unauthorized scanning, probing, or modification of .gov DNS records associated with state or local election infrastructure.

2. Identification, analysis, or remediation of “dangling DNS” records, “orphaned subdomains,” or “subdomain takeover” vulnerabilities affecting federal, state, or election-related government domains.

3. Analysis or discussion of IP addresses, IP ranges, or infrastructure later identified as Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) in the December 2016 DHS/FBI “Grizzly Steppe” Joint Analysis Report, particularly as they relate to:

   * State voter registration databases
   * Election-related web services
   * State or county-level government domains

4. Internal discussions or briefings regarding vulnerabilities in election-related digital infrastructure prior to or during the 2016 U.S. election cycle.

**Search Terms**

To assist in locating responsive records, I request that searches include combinations of the following terms:

“DNS”, “dangling DNS”, “subdomain takeover”, “orphaned domain”, “zone file”, “election infrastructure”, “voter database”, “VRDB”, “Grizzly Steppe”, “Russian IP”, “IOC”, “APT28”, “APT29”, “DHS alert”, “cyber intrusion election”, “state election system”, “Albert sensor”.

**Key Custodians**

Records involving the following officials are likely to be responsive, and I request that their email accounts and files be specifically searched:

* Michael Daniel (Special Assistant to the President and Cybersecurity Coordinator)
* Lisa Monaco (Assistant to the President for Homeland Security and Counterterrorism)
* Denis McDonough (White House Chief of Staff)
* Celeste Wallander (NSC Senior Director for Russia and Central Asia)

In addition, I request searches of records involving communications with:

* Jeh Johnson (Secretary of Homeland Security), where such communications are preserved within Executive Office of the President systems.

**Systems and Repositories**

Please include searches of:

* Email systems (including archived mailboxes)
* NSC and OSTP shared drives or collaboration platforms
* Records maintained by the White House Communications Agency (WHCA), where applicable
* Any preserved chat or messaging systems (specifically Slack) used for official communications

**Date Range**

January 1, 2014 through January 20, 2017.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of all reports, lists, databases, or individual documents created since 1/1/16 detail lawsuits brought against the city's police department. This should include any documentation mechanism your department uses to track lawsuits. It should also include any documents your department submits to local, state, or federal agencies listing the number and status of lawsuits brought against the police department. This request should include documents compiled quarterly, annually, or in other increments and documents or databases that contain responsive information over a longer period. 

- The records should include, if it exists, the following information: 
Settlement amounts
Case category (civil rights, collisions, etc) 
Case type/description (harassment, crowd control, in-custody death, etc)
Involved officers
Case numbers
Total expenses (this should include the legal costs to the city, including outsite litigation costs.) 

- Any corrective action reports that describe the incident that caused the lawsuit and the corrective actions taken to minimize risk, prevent recurrence, and hold the department and the involved officers accountable. The reports are often sent to respective bodies to approve settlements (city council, board of supervisors, controller, or risk management office) or created by another office within the city or county. 
- Copies of settlement agreements 

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv). California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.

title: Tyler County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tyler County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 24 2026

P.O. Box 66
Middlebourne, WV
26149

To Whom It May Concern, 

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to County Name opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Drew Solt
drewsolt@yahoo.com
740-243-6793

title: NY Department of Health Complaints against Ivy Medical, Athanasian 2024-26.

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY Department of Health Complaints against Ivy Medical, Athanasian 2024-26.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all complaints made about, regarding, or mentioning either "Ivy Medical" or the individual doctor "Edward Athanasian" during the date range March 1, 2024 to February 28, 2026.

In these records we would like to see the following, if available:
1. The text of the complaint, else a summary if text is not available.
2. Date complaint was filed
3. Current status of complaint / whether resolved
4. Amount in dispute, if regarding financial complaint
5. Date of service with the provider
6. Date of payment made to the provider, if available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tracie McMillan

title: Google Workspace Organizational Units and Microsoft Groups

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Google Workspace Organizational Units and Microsoft Groups
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The list and structure of the Organizational Units your agency has in your Google Workspace configuration, including sub-units. The easiest way to retrieve this is probably using GAM (https://github.com/GAM-team/GAM) by using the command "gam print orgs". Alternatively, it may be listed in the Google Admin console, but from my understanding it may be more difficult to retrieve the structure from that.

The list and structure of your Microsoft 365 Groups and Microsoft 365 Security Groups. This should be in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and can be exported to a csv.

To make things simple, I do not need a comprehensive list of every single individual and accounts that are in the Organizational Units or Groups. However, if it's included in the output I would still appreciate metadata such as group email addresses, owner information, permission information, etc.

If you have any questions or wish to clarify any part of this request feel free to let me know.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Redmond Police Department Flock Records Request

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Redmond Police Department Flock Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to public records concerning the use of the Flock Safety Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) system by the Redmond Police Department.

    I request the following records from January 1, 2024 to March 22, 2026:

    1. ALPR Search Audit Log: Please provide a copy of the complete search audit log, or equivalent electronic record, for all searches conducted by Redmond Police Department personnel in the Flock Safety system.

    If possible, please provide this log in a machine-readable format such as a CSV or Excel file. I request that this log include, but not be limited to, the following data fields for each individual search conducted:

        Date and time of the search
        The name, badge number, or unique identifier of the officer who conducted the search
        The justification or reason for the search (e.g., case number, specific crime type, “stolen vehicle,” etc.)
        The search parameters used (e.g., full or partial plate number, vehicle make/model, etc.)
        Whether the search produced any “hits” or results (a simple yes/no or a count of hits is sufficient).

    Clarification: To be clear, I am not requesting the photographic or location data returned by these searches. I am only requesting the administrative metadata about each search performed by city personnel.

    2. Policies and Procedures: Please provide a copy of all current policies, procedures, training manuals, and inter-agency agreements governing the use of the Flock Safety ALPR system by the Redmond Police Department.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey JJ Jr S.
